behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch recursion across OS events (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). An in-process mock watcher can miss real recursive-subdirectory event behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is only filesystem processing with no AI/model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against real temp input/output directories, create `input/subdir/new.txt`, and assert transformed output is produced for that nested file without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff: request intake + dedup store + configurable window application (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required for this specific failure mode, so Integration is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text is about duplicate processing requests within a window, not OS watcher semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake + dedup + pipeline modules with a controllable clock/config; submit the same file path twice within the configured window and assert only one pipeline execution occurs, then after window expiry assert processing is allowed again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events (REAL-INFRA). Mocked event sources can hide real watcher behavior and timing quirks (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output participates.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with a real watched directory; perform a write pattern that triggers multiple events (e.g., temp-write + rename/append) and assert exactly one processing/output record for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level directory lifecycle behavior (delete/recreate) while a live watcher is active, which requires real filesystem events and watcher reattachment behavior (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior contains no AI/ML steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real temp watch path, delete the watched directory, recreate it, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file arrival concurrency plus filesystem timestamp semantics/granularity, which can differ at OS/filesystem boundary (REAL-INFRA). A purely mocked queue may pass while real timestamp behavior fails (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because ordering is deterministic daemon logic, not model output.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files near-simultaneously in the real watched directory with controlled creation times and assert processing order matches creation timestamp order in observed outputs/logs.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in real write-in-progress timing/locking and event timing interactions at filesystem boundary (REAL-INFRA). In-process fakes often cannot reproduce partial-write visibility races (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI component is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file stream in the watched directory, write partial content and keep the stream open, confirm daemon does not process yet, then close stream and assert processing starts only after completion.
